title: BearerAuth and BasicAuth pre-compute HeaderValue at construction time, making auth_header() infallible and fast
description: BearerAuth::new() and BasicAuth::new() validate the credentials and construct the HeaderValue immediately, storing it in the struct. auth_header() then just clones this pre-built value — no allocation, no validation, no Result return type needed. The consequences of this design are: (1) invalid credentials (empty token, control characters, colon in username) fail at process startup before any network call is attempted; (2) auth_header() returns Option<(HeaderName, HeaderValue)> not Result<...>, so every call site in the client is free of error-handling boilerplate; (3) no allocation per request; (4) credentials are not re-processed on every call, reducing the surface where they might be accidentally logged. A contributor might suggest deferring construction to auth_header() to 'keep new() simple'. That would be wrong: it would push validation errors to the first network request (silent until then), require auth_header() to return a Result (propagating error handling to every HTTP call site), and re-do the base64 encoding on every request. Pre-computing at construction is the correct tradeoff.
close_reason: Already implemented: BearerAuth and BasicAuth store HeaderValue as field computed at construction
